skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Guides the final steps after implementation by verifying tests, presenting merge or pull request options, and handling branch cleanup.
skill.summary: Finish a development branch with tests, merge choices, pull request setup, and controlled cleanup.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command patterns, but most are Markdown examples for legitimate git and test workflows rather than hidden executable code. The skill does intentionally guide agents through mutating git actions, including merge, branch deletion, and worktree removal, so it should be published with a medium-risk warning rather than blocked.

medium_findings.title: Mutating Git Workflow Commands
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for external command use. The commands are documented git workflow steps, but they can change branches, merge code, delete branches, and remove worktrees if executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible Markdown instructions, not concealed code. Risk remains because the workflow directs real git operations that can alter or delete local repository state.

medium_findings.title: Pull Request Command Uses Shell Substitution
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for shell substitution pattern, with limited risk. The gh command uses command substitution to build a pull request body from a fixed heredoc template.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is present and would execute if copied into a shell. The heredoc body is static template content, so I did not find evidence of command injection or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Standard Device Redirection Is Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for filesystem risk. The referenced line redirects stderr to /dev/null while probing git merge bases and does not read or write user files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains standard shell error suppression for git branch detection. No evidence found of filesystem traversal, sensitive file access, or persistence.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptography. The flagged line is front matter text describing the skill and contains no cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a plain language description. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, cipher configuration, hashing code, or any cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains fenced shell examples for tests, git operations, GitHub CLI pull request creation, and worktree cleanup. These are expected for a branch-finishing workflow but should be treated as actions requiring repository context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicitly present in the skill file. Their purpose matches the skill description, and no hidden execution mechanism was found.

content.value_statement: Finishing a branch often requires tests, merge decisions, pull request setup, and cleanup in the right order. This skill gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a structured workflow for completing that process.
actual_capabilities: Requires test verification before offering completion choices.
Helps identify a likely base branch using git merge-base.
Presents four explicit choices: merge locally, create a pull request, keep the branch, or discard work.
Guides local merge, pull request creation, branch deletion, and worktree cleanup steps.
Requires typed confirmation before discarding branch work.
Documents common mistakes and red flags for branch completion.
limitations: It assumes the repository uses git and may use the GitHub CLI for pull requests.
It does not choose the correct test command without project context.
It does not resolve merge conflicts or failing tests by itself.
It can delete local branches or worktrees if the discard path is confirmed.

use_cases.title: Complete Feature Work
use_cases.description: Use the workflow after a feature passes tests and needs a clear merge or pull request decision.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reviewable Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Guide an assistant to push the current branch and create a pull request with summary and test plan sections.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Worktrees
use_cases.description: Remove finished branch worktrees only after the selected completion path makes cleanup appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Finish My Current Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to help me complete this branch. Verify tests first, then show the available completion choices.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill. If tests pass, help me push this branch and prepare a pull request with a concise summary and test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Locally After Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to verify the test suite, identify the base branch, merge this work locally, and rerun the tests.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Cleanup Choices
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to evaluate whether this branch should be merged, kept, or discarded. Require explicit confirmation before any destructive cleanup.

output_examples.input: The feature is complete and tests pass. What should happen next?
output_examples.output: Implementation complete. The assistant presents four choices: merge locally, push and create a pull request, keep the branch, or discard the work.

output_examples.input: I want to discard this branch.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the branch, commits, and worktree that would be deleted, then waits for the exact confirmation word before proceeding.
The assistant pushes the branch, prepares a pull request summary, includes a test plan, and preserves the worktree.

best_practices: Run the project test suite before presenting merge or pull request options.
Confirm the base branch before merging or deleting branch work.
Use the discard option only after reviewing the branch, commits, and worktree path.
anti_patterns: Do not merge or create a pull request when tests are failing.
Do not delete branches or worktrees without explicit user confirmation.
Do not force-push unless the user directly requests it.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when implementation is complete and you need to decide how to merge, review, keep, or discard the branch.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to verify tests first, but the exact command depends on the project.

faq.question: Can it create a pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a GitHub CLI workflow for pushing the branch and creating a pull request.

faq.question: Can it delete work?
faq.answer: Yes. The discard path can delete a branch and worktree, but the skill requires typed confirmation first.

faq.question: Does it support git worktrees?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks whether the branch is in a worktree and removes it only for selected cleanup paths.

faq.question: Is it safe for production repositories?
faq.answer: It is suitable with human review. Because it can run mutating git commands, review each action before execution.
